prompt: O governo Bolsonaro recusou ofertas de vacinas contra COVID-19 da Pfizer em 2020?
response:
**Yes, the Brazilian government under President Jair Bolsonaro rejected or ignored multiple offers from Pfizer for COVID-19 vaccines in 2020.**

### Key Evidence from Sources
- **Multiple Offers in August 2020**: Pfizer made three formal offers (August 14, 18, and 26) for 70 million doses each, with initial deliveries starting in December 2020 (e.g., 1.5 million doses by end of 2020 in later offers). The government did not respond within the 15-day deadlines, allowing them to expire without acceptance or rejection ([1](https://www.bbc.com/portuguese/brasil-57104347), [2](https://www12.senado.leg.br/noticias/materias/2021/05/13/representante-da-pfizer-confirma-governo-nao-respondeu-ofertas-feitas-em-agosto-de-2020), [4](https://g1.globo.com/politica/noticia/2021/05/13/governo-federal-nao-aceitou-propostas-que-previam-15-milhao-de-doses-de-vacina-ainda-em-2020-diz-pfizer.ghtml)).
- **Pricing and Terms**: Offered at $10 per dose (half the ~$20 paid by the US, UK, and EU), but deemed "aggressive" and too expensive by then-Health Minister Eduardo Pazuello, despite later approval at the same price ([3](https://www.brasildefato.com.br/2021/06/07/bolsonaro-recusou-vacina-da-pfizer-pela-metade-do-preco-pago-por-outros-pais/), [8](https://www.brasil247.com/brasil/bolsonaro-recusou-vacina-quando-a-pfizer-venderia-ao-brasil-com-50-de-desconto)).
- **Additional Rejections**: Up to 11 total refusals, including further Pfizer offers in November 2020 (70 million doses) and February 2021 (100 million), with no contract until March 2021 ([5](https://g1.globo.com/politica/blog/octavio-guedes/post/2021/04/27/cpi-da-covid-governo-bolsonaro-recusou-11-vezes-ofertas-para-compras-de-vacina.ghtml), [4], [9](https://www.terra.com.br/noticias/brasil/rejeicao-de-70-milhoes-de-doses-da-pfizer-por-gestao-bolsonaro-sera-novo-foco-da-cpi-da-covid,acf06df55f3032fad814bfe054e8eb80iwjr5udu.html)).
- **Testimony Confirmation**: Pfizer executive Carlos Murillo testified to the CPI da Covid in May 2021 that the government ignored these offers, despite early contacts in May-June 2020 ([1], [2], [4]).

### Government Justifications
Pazuello cited contract clauses (e.g., no liability for side effects, no tech transfer), small initial volumes, and high cost—issues overcome later for the same terms. Bolsonaro publicly rejected Chinese vaccines like CoronaVac in October 2020 ([6](https://g1.globo.com/ciencia-e-saude/noticia/2021/05/28/vacinas-teriam-salvo-95-mil-vidas-se-governo-bolsonaro-nao-tivesse-ignorado-ofertas-calcula-pesquisador.ghtml), [7](https://www.bbc.com/portuguese/brasil-56746026), [9]).

### Impact
Researchers estimated these delays cost ~95,000 lives by March 2021, as 4.5 million doses could have arrived earlier ([6]).

All sources (BBC, G1, Senado, Brasil de Fato) align on the facts from CPI hearings and documents, with no contradictions. First Pfizer contract signed March 2021.
